reasoning: The skeleton has been tried 6 times with scores flat/declining (-136.4, -139.1, -136.8, -136.4, -126.8, 3.7). The latest iteration (score=3.7, episode=1000) shows the agent survives but does nothing useful (per-step reward is only 0.004). The fundamental design is broken: (1) pos_change and ang_change use abs(o[i])-abs(n[i]) which is undirected and can reward moving away from zero, (2) vel_change uses squared differences which penalizes ANY velocity change, (3) contact_bonus uses difference of sums which is noisy, (4) action_cost is too harsh, (5) exploration_bonus is tiny. Need a complete rebuild with simpler, survival-focused components: survival bonus, gentle movement encouragement, and minimal action penalties.
